svg.text: Denaria PerpPair — on-chain architecture after the Arbitrum Stylus port
Engine ported to Rust/WASM (Stylus); manager/Vault/oracle AND the math/preview libraries stay Solidity. Bit-exact (differential harness + golden vectors).
views: getPrice · calcPnL · getLpLiquidityBalance · ReadParameters/Fees · Vault getters · read-parity getters (curveParameters · funding) · governance
u8/u32/u64/bool packed · WAD/accumulators full-width · M (2×2) + row G flattened to scalars
trade+funding · liquidity · close+PnL · liquidation+auto-close replayed bit-exact vs real PerpPair · invariants + fuzz
Same math as the deployed Solidity libraries (right) — golden-vector-locked, so quotes and execution agree bit-exactly.
Engine ↔ crate: a normal in-WASM call (the standalone crate also deploys on its own with its own #[entrypoint]).
→ each crosses the WASM boundary (≈+24k pedestal)
reads engine state via the read-parity getters
same ABI as the legacy system — FE migration = address swap
DELEGATECALL: calcMR inside removeCollateral's margin guard
EVM linked-library mechanism — not available to a Stylus program
bit-exact twins of the engine's embedded Rust crate
Legend / what changed vs the Solidity baseline
Solidity math libraries (FE quotes + Vault-linked, bit-exact twins)
Baseline: a monolithic Solidity PerpPair inheritance chain (perpModules/ + storage/).
Now: one Stylus WASM contract (1 #[entrypoint]/1 #[public] impl) + 9 internal Rust modules; storage repacked.
